proposal_text: This project proposes to develop a conversational AI chatbot to provide initial mental health support triage for university students. The chatbot will conduct a structured conversational assessment to evaluate symptoms of anxiety and depression using validated clinical screening instruments (PHQ-9, GAD-7) adapted into conversational format. Based on the assessment, the chatbot will recommend one of three pathways: self-help resources, a scheduled counselling appointment, or immediate crisis intervention referral. The chatbot will store conversation logs for quality improvement purposes. Student data will be pseudonymised but linked to student records to enable follow-up. The chatbot will include a disclaimer that it is not a substitute for professional mental health care. The system has not been validated against clinical assessments performed by qualified professionals. Conversations will be processed by an external cloud-based NLP service.

assessment.risk_summary: The proposed conversational AI chatbot for mental health support triage poses risks to student data privacy, potential biases in clinical screening instruments, and lack of validation against professional assessments. The system's reliance on an external cloud-based NLP service also raises security concerns.

identified_risks.explanation: The chatbot will store conversation logs, including pseudonymised student data linked to student records.
The PHQ-9 and GAD-7 instruments may perpetuate biases if not adapted carefully.

identified_risks.risk: Lack of validation against professional assessments
identified_risks.explanation: The chatbot's recommendations are based on unvalidated clinical screening instruments.

identified_risks.risk: Security risks from external NLP service
identified_risks.explanation: The system relies on an external cloud-based NLP service, which may introduce security vulnerabilities.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Research must contribute to society and human well-being — discuss how applications may harm individuals, groups or society and ways to mitigate such harms (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Discuss potential harms and mitigation strategies

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Avoid harm — consider how data, code or other outcomes of research may cause harm (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Consider potential harms from data and code

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Respect privacy — obtain informed consent where required and protect personally identifiable information (Mandatory)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Obtain informed consent for data collection

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Data protection by design and default (Article 25 EU AI Act)
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Implement data protection by design and default

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: The chatbot's collection of student data may infringe on students' right to private life
The chatbot's handling of student data must comply with data protection principles

historical_precedents.lesson: Be cautious when deploying language models to avoid perpetuating biases and generating harmful content
Consider the potential consequences of using AI-powered tools, especially those that may infringe on individuals' rights

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Implement robust data protection measures, including encryption and secure storage
Regularly audit and validate the chatbot's performance against professional assessments

tier1_mandatory_actions: Obtain informed consent for data collection
Implement data protection by design and default
tier2_reflective_prompts: Consider potential biases in clinical screening instruments
Reflect on the chatbot's potential impact on students' mental health
